text: The surgeon performs the procedure using an anterior transperitoneal approach, incising the peritoneum at the intersection of the medial umbilical ligament and the vas deferens. The paravesical space is dissected until the endopelvic fascia is exposed. Finally, the urachus is coagulated and cut.

The surgeon performs a longitudinal cystostomy to open the bladder at the prostate-vesicle junction. A stitch is placed in the middle lobe to enhance traction, while the bilateral ureteric orifices are identified to prevent injury.

The surgeon develops the plane between the prostatic adenoma and the pseudocapsule using sharp and blunt dissection, starting at the six o'clock position and moving towards the right lobe. Subsequently, the plane between the left adenoma lobe and the pseudocapsule is developed. At the apex, the prostatic urethra is transected, and the prostatic adenoma is completely dissected.

The surgeon performs a single-layer running suture from the urethra to the bladder neck to retrigonize the prostatic fossa after hemostasis. Subsequently, the cystotomy is closed with a double-layer running suture, and a three-way catheter is inserted for continuous irrigation.
The surgeon begins the procedure with a posterior midline cystostomy, providing direct access to the large prostate adenoma without needing to mobilize the bladder.

The surgeon retracts the bladder using three exovulporal sutures. The bilateral ureteric orifices are identified to prevent injury. An incision is made at the six o'clock position of the bladder neck to expose the prostatic adenoma. The plane between the prostatic adenoma and the pseudocapsule is developed using both sharp and blunt dissection techniques.

The surgeon develops the plane circumferentially around both the right and left sides of the prostatic adenoma. After completing the dissection, the prostatic fossa is inspected for hemostasis, and single stitches are placed to prevent post-operative bleeding.
The surgeon places a three-way catheter with continuous irrigation into the prostatic fossa. The cystostomy incision is closed using a single layer of single stitches.
